children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is hereby established the Technical Guidelines Development Committee (hereafter in this subpart referred to as the “Development Committee”).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Development Committee shall assist the Executive Director of the Commission in the development of the voluntary voting system guidelines.

children.heading: Deadline for initial set of recommendations
children.label: 2 Deadline for initial set of recommendations
children.text: The Development Committee shall provide its first set of recommendations under this section to the Executive Director of the Commission not later than 9 months after all of its members have been appointed.

children.label: i
children.text: Members of the Standards Board.

children.label: ii
children.text: Members of the Board of Advisors.

children.label: iii
children.text: Members of the Architectural and Transportation Barrier Compliance Board under .section 792 of title 29

children.label: B
children.text: A representative of the American National Standards Institute.

children.label: C
children.text: A representative of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers.

children.label: D
children.text: Two representatives of the National Association of State Election Directors selected by such Association who are not members of the Standards Board or Board of Advisors, and who are not of the same political party.

children.label: E
children.text: Other individuals with technical and scientific expertise relating to voting systems and voting equipment.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: 2 Quorum
children.text: A majority of the members of the Development Committee shall constitute a quorum, except that the Development Committee may not conduct any business prior to the appointment of all of its members.

children.heading: No compensation for service
children.label: d No compensation for service
children.text: Members of the Development Committee shall not receive any compensation for their service, but shall be paid travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5, while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Development Committee.

children.heading: Technical support from National Institute of Standards and Technology
children.label: e Technical support from National Institute of Standards and Technology

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: At the request of the Development Committee, the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology shall provide the Development Committee with technical support necessary for the Development Committee to carry out its duties under this part.

children.label: A
children.text: the security of computers, computer networks, and computer data storage used in voting systems, including the computerized list required under ;section 21083(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: methods to detect and prevent fraud;

children.label: C
children.text: the protection of voter privacy;

children.label: D
children.text: the role of human factors in the design and application of voting systems, including assistive technologies for individuals with disabilities (including blindness) and varying levels of literacy; and

children.label: E
children.text: remote access voting, including voting through the Internet.

children.heading: No private sector intellectual property rights in guidelines
children.label: 3 No private sector intellectual property rights in guidelines
children.text: No private sector individual or entity shall obtain any intellectual property rights to any guideline or the contents of any guideline (or any modification to any guideline) adopted by the Commission under this chapter.

children.heading: Publication of recommendations in Federal Register
children.label: f Publication of recommendations in Federal Register
children.text: At the time the Commission adopts any voluntary voting system guideline pursuant to , the Development Committee shall cause to have published in the Federal Register the recommendations it provided under this section to the Executive Director of the Commission concerning the guideline adopted.section 20962 of this title
